test.failureDetail: Test 'glyph_cache_keyed_on_physical_pixel_size' in scene_render.rs:1110 causes stack overflow (SIGABRT). GlyphCache::zeroed() returns a ~1.3 MiB struct by value and the test allocates two on the stack, exceeding the default ~2 MiB thread stack. Other tests in the file use zeroed_glyph_cache() helper which Box-allocates. All other 1554 tests pass.

reviewsSummary.note: Feature review not performed due to test failure

blockingIssues.description: Test 'glyph_cache_keyed_on_physical_pixel_size' (scene_render.rs:1110) stack overflows: GlyphCache::zeroed() returns ~1.3 MiB by value, test creates two on the stack (~2.6 MiB total), exceeding default thread stack size. Fix: the first half of the test only checks size_px field values (24 != 32) — it doesn't need a full GlyphCache at all. Remove the GlyphCache::zeroed() calls and just assert 24 != 32 directly, OR use Box allocation like the zeroed_glyph_cache() helper used by all other tests in the file.
